introduction: OpenAI has been at the forefront of artificial intelligence research and development, and its ChatGPT platform has been a key driver of this growth. With the introduction of new features and products, OpenAI is continuing to push the boundaries of what is possible with AI. This report provides an overview of the new features and products from OpenAI's ChatGPT, including GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.1, and the updates to ChatGPT's default model. It also examines the strategic significance of these developments and their potential impact on the market.
strategic_significance: The introduction of new features and products from OpenAI's ChatGPT has significant strategic implications for the company and the broader AI market. The advanced agentic coding model, GPT-5.2-Codex, has the potential to revolutionize the field of software engineering, while the upgrade to the GPT-5 series, GPT-5.1, will enable more conversational and smarter interactions with ChatGPT. The strategic partnership with Amazon will also help OpenAI to expand its reach and scale its AI infrastructure.

The research scope includes an analysis of the new features and products from OpenAI's ChatGPT, as well as the strategic partnership with Amazon and the updates to ChatGPT's default model.

The report also includes a cross-source analysis and a summary of the key findings and takeaways.
executive_summary: OpenAI has introduced several new features and products, including GPT-5.2-Codex, an advanced agentic coding model, and GPT-5.1, an upgrade to the GPT-5 series. The company has also announced a strategic partnership with Amazon and has updated ChatGPT's default model to better recognize and support people in moments of distress. Additionally, OpenAI has expanded its audio capabilities through its API and has strengthened ChatGPT's responses in sensitive conversations. With over 900M weekly active users and 50 million consumer subscribers, OpenAI is scaling its AI infrastructure to meet growing demand.

official_insights.title: Introducing GPT-5.2-Codex
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.2-Codex, an advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity. The model is optimized for long-horizon work, large code changes, and Windows environments, and has stronger cybersecurity capabilities than previous models. It is available for paid ChatGPT users and will be rolled out to API users in the coming weeks.
official_insights.detailed_summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.2-Codex, an advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity. The model is optimized for long-horizon work, large code changes, and Windows environments, and has stronger cybersecurity capabilities than previous models. It is available for paid ChatGPT users and will be rolled out to API users in the coming weeks.
official_insights.reasoning: The release of GPT-5.2-Codex demonstrates OpenAI's continued advancement in the field of artificial intelligence, particularly in the areas of software engineering and cybersecurity. The model's improved performance on benchmarks such as SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench 2.0 suggests its potential to revolutionize the way software is developed and maintained.

official_insights.title: GPT-5.1: A smarter, more conversational ChatGPT
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI is releasing GPT-5.1, an upgrade to the GPT-5 series, which includes GPT-5.1 Instant and GPT-5.1 Thinking models. GPT-5.1 Instant is now warmer and more conversational by default, while GPT-5.1 Thinking is easier to understand and faster on simple tasks. The update also introduces more intuitive controls for shaping ChatGPT's tone.

official_insights.title: Strengthening ChatGPT’s responses in sensitive conversations
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has updated ChatGPT's default model to better recognize and support people in moments of distress, with the help of over 170 mental health experts. The updates focus on mental health concerns, self-harm, suicide, and emotional reliance on AI, with a goal of providing a supportive space for users and guiding them to reach out to professionals when needed.
official_insights.detailed_summary: OpenAI has updated ChatGPT's default model to better recognize and support people in moments of distress, with the help of over 170 mental health experts. The updates focus on mental health concerns, self-harm, suicide, and emotional reliance on AI, with a goal of providing a supportive space for users and guiding them to reach out to professionals when needed.
official_insights.reasoning: The updates are based on a five-step process that includes defining the problem, measuring it, validating the approach, mitigating risks, and continuing to measure and iterate. The process involves building and refining detailed guides, called taxonomies, to teach the model to respond more appropriately to sensitive conversations.
key_metrics: 65-80% reduction in responses that fall short of desired behavior
Over 170 mental health experts involved in the update process
key_features: Improved recognition of signs of distress
More empathetic and safe responses to potential signs of delusion or mania
Guidance towards professional care when appropriate
Gentle reminders to take breaks during long sessions
Updated Model Spec with explicit goals for model behavior

references.title: Introducing GPT-5.2-Codex - OpenAI
references.snippet:
Switch to

 ChatGPT(opens in a new window)
 Sora(opens in a new window)
 API Platform(opens in a new window)

OpenAI

December 18, 2025

ProductRelease

# Introducing GPT-5.2-Codex

The most advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity.

$ npm i -g @openai/codex

Today we’re releasing GPT‑5.2-Codex, the most advanced agentic coding model yet for complex, real-world software engineering. GPT‑5.2-Codex is a version of GPT‑5.2⁠ further optimized for agentic coding in Codex, including improvements on long-horizon work through context compaction, stronger performance on large code changes like refactors and migrations, improved performance in Windows environments, and significantly stronger cybersecurity capabilities. [...] We're releasing GPT‑5.2-Codex today in all Codex surfaces for paid ChatGPT users, and working towards safely enabling access to GPT‑5.2-Codex for API users in the coming weeks. In parallel, we’re piloting invite-only trusted access to upcoming capabilities and more permissive models for vetted professionals and organizations focused on defensive cybersecurity work. We believe that this approach to deployment will balance accessibility with safety.

## Pushing the frontier on real-world software engineering [...] GPT‑5.2-Codex achieves state-of-the-art performance on SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench 2.0, benchmarks designed to test agentic performance on a wide variety of tasks in realistic terminal environments. It is also much more effective and reliable at agentic coding in native Windows environments, building on capabilities introduced in GPT‑5.1-Codex-Max.

With these improvements, Codex is more capable at working in large repositories over extended sessions with full context intact. It can more reliably complete complex tasks like large refactors, code migrations, and feature builds — continuing to iterate without losing track, even when plans change or attempts fail.

references.title: GPT-5.1: A smarter, more conversational ChatGPT - OpenAI
references.snippet:
Earlier this year, we added preset options to tailor the tone of how ChatGPT responds. Today, we’re refining those options to better reflect the most common ways people use ChatGPT. Default, Friendly(formerly Listener), and Efficient (formerly Robot) remain (with updates), and we’re adding Professional, Candid, and Quirky. These options are designed to align with what we’ve learned about how people naturally steer the model, making it quick and intuitive to choose a personality that feels uniquely right. [...] What's next

Today’s GPT‑5.1 updates and new customization options are a step toward a ChatGPT that feels like it fits you—smarter, more enjoyable to talk to, and more adaptable to your preferences. Going forward, we’ll continue improving along these dimensions—there’s much more to come.

   2025
   ChatGPT

Author

OpenAI

Technical Report: Performance and baseline evaluations of gpt-oss-safeguard-120b and gpt-oss-safeguard-20b Safety Oct 29, 2025 [...] The updated styles and tone options are rolling out today, and the ability to finetune specific characteristics is starting to roll out gradually later this week as an experiment, starting with a limited number of users. Both will continue to improve over time. Additionally, the updated GPT‑5.1 models are also better at adhering to custom instructions, giving you even more precise control over tone and behavior.

Updates you make in personalization settings now take effect across all chats right away, including ongoing conversations, so your experience stays consistent. Before, changes to base style and tone or custom instructions only applied to conversations started afterward.

What's next

references.title: Scaling AI for everyone - OpenAI
references.snippet:
ChatGPT is where people start with AI, with more than 900M weekly active users, and we now have more than 50 million consumer subscribers.. Subscriber momentum accelerated meaningfully to start the year, with January and February on track to be the largest months for new subscribers in our history. People use ChatGPT to learn, write, plan, and build. As usage scales, the product improves in ways people feel immediately: faster responses, higher reliability, stronger safety, and more consistent performance. [...] You can see that scale in our products. Codex brings the power of a top engineer to anyone who wants to build software. Weekly Codex users have more than tripled since the start of the year to 1.6M. More people are now creating, automating, and shipping software that once required a full engineering team.

More than 9 million paying business users rely on ChatGPT for work, and startups, enterprises, and governments are building on the OpenAI platform to transform how their products and services are designed, delivered, and run. Teams start with individual productivity and move quickly to deployment across engineering, support, finance, sales, and operations. Our Frontier platform helps enterprises build, deploy, and manage AI coworkers. [...] Image 3: Amazon > Card

references.title: Strengthening ChatGPT’s responses in sensitive conversations - OpenAI
references.snippet:
Loading…

Share

We recently updated ChatGPT’s default model⁠(opens in a new window) to better recognize and support people in moments of distress. Today we’re sharing how we made those improvements and how they are performing. Working with mental health experts who have real-world clinical experience, we’ve taught the model to better recognize distress, de-escalate conversations, and guide people toward professional care when appropriate. We’ve also expanded access to crisis hotlines, re-routed⁠(opens in a new window) sensitive conversations originating from other models to safer models, and added gentle reminders to take breaks⁠ during long sessions. [...] We’ve continued improving GPT‑5’s reliability in long conversations. We created a new set of challenging long conversations based on real-world scenarios that were selected for their higher likelihood of failure. We estimate that our latest models maintained over 95% reliability in longer conversations, improving in a particularly challenging setting we’ve mentioned before⁠. [...] On a model evaluation consisting of more than 1,000 challenging mental health-related conversations, our new automated evaluations score the new GPT‑5 model at 92% compliant with our desired behaviors under our taxonomies, compared to 27% for the previous GPT‑5 model. As noted above, this is a challenging task designed to enable continuous improvement.

Weekly Codex users have more than tripled since the start of the year to 1.6M. More people are now creating, automating, and shipping software that once required a full engineering team.

More than 9 million paying business users rely on ChatGPT for work, and startups, enterprises, and governments are building on the OpenAI platform to transform how their products and services are designed, delivered, and run. Teams start with individual productivity and move quickly to deployment across engineering, support, finance, sales, and operations. Our Frontier platform helps enterprises build, deploy, and manage AI coworkers.

references.title: Strengthening ChatGPT’s responses in sensitive conversations - OpenAI
references.snippet:
from evaluations that are designed to not “saturate” near perfect performance, and error rates are not representative of average production traffic. [...] We’ve continued improving GPT‑5’s reliability in long conversations. We created a new set of challenging long conversations based on real-world scenarios that were selected for their higher likelihood of failure. We estimate that our latest models maintained over 95% reliability in longer conversations, improving in a particularly challenging setting we’ve mentioned before⁠. [...] Similar to our work on HealthBench⁠, we collaborated with the Global Physician Network to produce targeted evaluations that we use internally to assess model performance in mental health contexts, including in new models prior to release.

Looking ahead

This work is deeply important to us, and we’re grateful to the many mental health experts around the world who continue to guide it. We’ve made meaningful progress, but there’s more to do. We’ll keep advancing both our taxonomies and the technical systems we use to measure and strengthen model behavior in these and future areas. Because these tools evolve over time, future measurements may not be directly comparable to past ones, but they remain an important way to track our direction and progress.
